item.title: Speedy trial demanded for violence against women, children
item.description: A group of civil society citizens, including rights activists and politicians, on Thursday demanded that the government enforce a zero-tolerance policy, ensure speedy trials and ensure the fair disposal of cases to end violence against women and children....

item.title: Bangladesh Navy-made floating crane joins fleet
item.description: A 70-tonne floating crane named BNFC Baliyan, made by the Bangladesh Navy-operated Dockyard and Engineering Works Limited, was handed over to the navy on Thursday....

item.title: First-week footfall drops for Eid films
item.description: According to hall owners and industry insiders speaking on Wednesday, Eid films experienced a lower turnout during their opening week....

item.title: Dissemination workshop on nanotechnology held at DU
item.description: The department of chemistry of Dhaka University and the DU Nanotechnology Centre jointly held a dissemination workshop at the seminar room of the DU Mukarram Hussain Khundker Bigyan Bhaban on Thursday....

item.title: UGC, UN Women to work to address gender-based violence at univs
item.description: The University Grants Commission of Bangladesh and the United Nations Women Bangladesh have jointly taken an initiative to undertake new activities to address sexual and gender-based violence in the country’s higher education institutions....

item.title: No alternative to following Zia’ path: law minister
item.description: Law, justice and parliamentary affairs minister Md Asaduzzaman on Thursday said that there was no alternative to following the path shown by former president Ziaur Rahman to build Bangladesh as an independent, sovereign and prosperous country....

item.title: Khalilur hints he may continue both roles concurrently
item.description: President-elect of the UN General Assembly’s 81st session and Bangladesh foreign minister Khalilur Rahman on Thursday indicated he may continue to discharge his responsibilities in both capacities concurrently, citing the example Humayun Rasheed Chowdhury, the last Bangladeshi......

item.title: Slight fall in temperature likely from today
item.description: Day and night temperatures are likely to fall slightly across the country from today, although rising humidity may continue to cause heat-related discomfort, according to the Bangladesh Meteorological Department....

item.title: BB to set loan approval cap in Tk 60,000cr package
item.description: Bangladesh Bank is considering imposing limits on large loans approvals under its newly announced Tk 60,000 crore stimulus package by linking loan amounts to borrowers’ assets and annual sales in an effort to prevent excessive lending and reduce the risk of misuse......

item.title: CPD slams US move to impose additional tariffs
item.description: Centre for Policy Dialogue distinguished fellow Mustafizur Rahman said on Thursday that the United States was trying to impose additional tariffs on the pretext of child and forced labour in the import chain, which would harm Bangladesh’s exports......

item.title: Bengali identity needs neither validation from Kolkata nor Muslim identity from Karachi: info minister
item.description: Information and broadcasting minister Zahir Uddin Swapan stated on Thursday that Bengali identity requires no validation from Kolkata, nor does Muslim identity necessitate approval from Rawalpindi or Karachi, emphasising Bangladesh's unique national character, reports BSS....

item.title: BB launches Tk 20,000cr stimulus fund
item.description: Bangladesh Bank on Thursday launched a Tk 20,000 crore pre-finance scheme under its Tk 60,000 crore stimulus package, capping loans to a single company or business group at Tk 200 crore to prevent excessive concentration of funds and ensure wider access to financing......

item.title: Swechchhasebak Dal leader beaten in Rangpur police station
item.description: Five police personnel, including the officer-in-charge of Rangpur Metropolitan Police’s kotwali police station, were withdrawn on Thursday and attached to the police lines over allegations that a leader of the Bangladesh Nationalist Party’s volunteer wing, Jatiyatabadi Swechchhasebak Dal......

item.title: Semiconductor roadshow in US begins today
item.description: The Bangladesh Semiconductor Industry Association is set to kick off ‘USA Roadshow 2026’ today, as part of the Silicon River and BRAINGAIN initiatives, aiming to enhance Bangladesh’s connection with the global semiconductor ecosystem......

item.title: 5 Bangladeshis injured in Kuwait airport drone attack
item.description: Five Bangladeshi nationals were injured in a drone attack on Kuwait International Airport in the early hours of Wednesday....

item.title: Govt to hold roadshows to attract FDI
item.description: The government will soon organise roadshows to attract local and foreign investments in closed and unprofitable state-owned factories......

item.title: Stocks extend rally for 9th session
item.description: Stocks extended its winning streak for a ninth consecutive trading session on Thursday, supported by renewed investor confidence following the implementation of commission reforms and the appointment of a new leadership team at the Bangladesh Securities and Exchange Commission (BSEC)......

item.title: Iran leader says dealt enemies ‘decisive blow’ in war
item.description: Iran’s supreme leader said Thursday that the United States and Israel had been dealt a ‘decisive blow’ in the Middle East war, after the government reported ‘no tangible progress’ in negotiations on ending the conflict....

item.title: India sees Venezuelan oil ‘opportunity’
item.description: India’s prime minister Narendra Modi hosted Venezuela’s interim president Delcy Rodriguez on Thursday for talks to bolster trade between the oil exporter and his energy-hungry nation......

item.title: China, Mideast war weigh on global steel sector: OECD
item.description: The global steel sector remains in crisis, the OECD said Thursday, as China subsidising production has resulted in a flood of cheap steel on the markets while the war in the Middle East has dented demand......

item.title: Tim Berners-Lee calls for AI to preserve ‘original values’ of web
item.description: World Wide Web pioneer Tim Berners-Lee says he wants to see artificial intelligence preserve ‘the original values’ of his invention while allowing users to filter personal data sent to tech giants......

item.title: Kremlin says Europe needs Russian oil and gas to ‘survive’
item.description: The Kremlin’s economic envoy told AFP on Thursday that Europe needed Russian oil and gas to ‘survive’ the energy crisis triggered by the war in the Middle East......

item.title: BNP, front orgs with no council sessions for long
item.description: The Bangladesh Nationalist Party and most of its affiliated and associate bodies keep functioning through expired committees without holding regular councils, increasing frustration among their leaders and activists over the party’s failure to ensure internal democracy and follow its constitution....

item.title: Indonesian rupiah falls to record low against US dollar
item.description: Indonesia’s rupiah hit more than 18,000 per dollar for the first time Thursday as the country is stung by surging energy costs, while lawmakers passed a bill expanding oversight of the central bank that raised concerns over its independence......

item.title: Apex Footwear wins 3 accolades at Retail Asia Awards
item.description: Apex Footwear Limited has recently earned three awards at the Retail Asia Awards 2026, marking its third consecutive year of recognition at the event, said a press release......

item.title: Janata Bank observes 45th death anniv of Ziaur Rahman
item.description: A discussion meeting and doa mahfil were held at Janata Bank PLC’s head office on Wednesday on the occasion of the 45th death anniversary of late president and Bangladesh Nationalist Party founder Ziaur Rahman, said a press release......

item.title: Govt should, rather, treat power-sector malaise
item.description: THE increase in retail power price by Tk 1.52 a unit or a kilowatt-hour, or 16.68 per cent, as the Energy Regulatory Commission announced on June 3, coming into force on June 1, raises concern amidst fears of a further impact on the majority of people already hit hard by persistent inflation. The commission has also announced an increase in bulk power price and......

item.title: A likely new ploy to delay Sagar-Runi trial
item.description: THE latest explanation offered in the long-running investigation into the murders of journalist couple Sagar Sarowar and Meherun Runi is as shocking as unacceptable. The attorney general informed the victims’ family on June 3 that the investigators were facing difficulties in tracing several police, Rapid Action Battalion and other officials involved in initial stages of the......

item.title: Decoupling war and politics from climate action
item.description: TODAY humanity is at a crossroads in the climate change crisis. Like every year, World Environment Day 2026, hosted by Azerbaijan in partnership with the UN Environment Programme (UNEP), will focus on the theme ‘Inspired by Nature. For Climate. For Our Future’. The theme emphasises the crucial intersection of urbanisation, nature-based solutions and climate......

item.title: Building flood resilience
item.description: FLOODS are not new to Bangladesh. For generations, our rivers have nourished agriculture, supported trade, and shaped the lives of millions. Yet the same rivers that sustain us can also bring immense hardship when they overflow during the monsoon season. As one of Bangladesh’s greatest environmental challenges, flooding continues to affect communities, ecosystems, infrastructure, and livelihoods across the country......

item.title: World Environment Day no marketing campaign
item.description: THERE is a ritual that plays out with clockwork precision every year in Bangladesh. On the eve of World Environment Day, press releases flood journalists’ inboxes. Corporate logos are affixed to leafy backdrops. Executives in crisp shirts kneel in the mud, just long enough for the camera shutter, and plant a sapling. By evening, the posts are up on LinkedIn. By June 6, the.....

item.title: Language movement and Mahbubul Haque
item.description: THE Bangla language movement is the most significant event in our national history. Mahbubul Haque, a memorable figure in our national history, was involved in the language movement from the very beginning......

item.title: Benfica say Real Madrid must pay 15m euros to sign Mourinho
item.description:
Portuguese giants Benfica confirmed on Thursday that Real Madrid would have to pay 15 million euros to prise away their coach Jose Mourinho.
...

item.title: Diallo gives Ivory Coast shock win over France
item.description: Amad Diallo scored a late winner as Ivory Coast stunned France 2-1 in a World Cup warm-up match in Nantes on Thursday....

item.title: Qualifier Chwalinska sets up Andreeva French Open final clash
item.description:
Maja Chwalinska made French Open history on Thursday as the world number 114 became the first qualifier in the professional era to reach the Roland Garros final, where she will take on Mirra Andreeva.
...

item.title: Pakistan upstage Australia for 2-1 ODI series win
item.description:
Spearhead Shaheen Shah Afridi and Shadab Khan starred in Pakistan's four-wicket win in their decisive third and final one-day international against Australia in Lahore on Thursday.
...

item.title: Robinson sparks New Zealand collapse after England slump
item.description: England's Ollie Robinson marked his first Test for more than two years with four wickets, including three in a sensational opening over, as New Zealand collapsed to 61-6 at stumps at Lord's on Thursday....

item.title: Liverpool appoint Spaniard Iraola as new boss
item.description: Liverpool named Andoni Iraola as their new manager on Thursday after sacking Arne Slot following a calamitous Premier League title defence....

item.title: Dilara’s fastest fifty guides Bangladesh to beat Netherlands
item.description:
Dilara Akter’s fastest fifty propelled Bangladesh to avenge their earlier loss as they beat the Netherlands by 13 runs in a rain-affected game in the Women’s Tri-Nation T20 series at the Grange Cricket Club Ground in Edinburgh, Scotland, on Thursday.
...

item.title: Zelensky for full ceasefire, face-to-face meeting with Putin
item.description: The Ukrainian president, Volodymyr Zelensky, proposed a face-to-face meeting with Vladimir Putin in a rare open letter to the Russian president on Thursday, saying he was also ready for a ‘full ceasefire’......

item.title: Section 144 imposed after Mymensingh land clash
item.description: Local administration on Thursday imposed emergency section 144 in Nandail upazila of Mymensingh after a clash that broke out between two groups over a land dispute....

item.title: Five die after drinking ‘toxic alcohol’ at Mithapukur
item.description: 5 people have died after drinking expired and toxic alcohol at Mithapukur in Rangpur....

item.title: Free uniforms, shoes for primary students by August: Bobby Hajjaj
item.description: The government is planning to gradually provide free uniforms and shoes to around 1.07 crore government primary school students across the country, said state minister for primary and mass education Bobby Hajjaj....

item.title: Share of education in GDP 2pc for coming FYs: edn minister
item.description: Education minister ANM Ehsanul Hoque Milon on Thursday said that the share of education in gross domestic product in the national budget for 2026–27 financial year will be increased to 2 per cent....

item.title: University student drowns in Jamalpur pond
item.description: A university student drowned while bathing in a pond in Jamalpur Thursday afternoon....

item.title: 62 injured in clash over local dominance in Jhenaidah
item.description: At least 62 people were injured in a violent clash between two rival groups over establishing supremacy at Shailkupa upazila of Jhenaidah on Thursday morning....

item.title: Cox’s Bazar landslide kills 2 workers
item.description: Two construction workers were killed and another was injured after being buried under a landslide during the construction of a bridge at Dariyanagar area of Cox’s Bazar Thursday afternoon....

item.title: RMCH CHILDREN’S ICU: Study finds high prevalence of multidrug-resistant bacteria
item.description: A recent study conducted at Rajshahi Medical College Hospital has found a high prevalence of multidrug-resistant bacteria among children admitted to its paediatric intensive care unit, raising concerns about the growing challenge of antimicrobial resistance in critical care settings....

item.title: 22 agroecology champions honoured in Rajshahi
item.description: Twenty-two farmers, environmental activists and grassroots organisers from the drought-prone Barind region were honoured on Thursday for their contributions to agroecology, biodiversity conservation and sustainable farming practices....

item.title: Special allocation of Tk 50,000 crore for haor areas demanded
item.description: Leaders of the Environment and Haor Development Organisation on Thursday demanded a special allocation of Tk 50,000 crore for development of the haor region in the upcoming national budget for the 2026-27 financial year....

item.title: Man held over assaulting pregnant wife for dowry in Chuadanga
item.description: A MAN has been arrested in Chuadanga’s Jibannagar upazila after his seven-month pregnant wife accused him of physically assaulting her over dowry demands, alleging that the abuse resulted in the death of their unborn child....

item.title: One arrested over rape of textile mill worker in Narsingdi
item.description: The police on Thursday arrested a suspect in a case filed over the rape of a 16-year old girl in Narsingdi....

item.title: Pakistan accuses India of weaponising water after treaty suspension
item.description: Pakistan said on Thursday two river projects by arch-rival India would ‘weaponise’ water and violate a major treaty between the neighbouring nations, threatening a response if they move ahead....

item.title: Ctg child rape: Police submit charge sheet
item.description: The police on Thursday submitted a charge sheet against a man in a case filed over the rape of a four-year-old child in Chattogram city....

item.title: Cabinet approves FDI incentive policy, BMU amendment law
item.description: The cabinet on Thursday approved the draft Foreign Direct Investment (FDI) Incentive Scheme Policy 2026 and the Bangladesh Medical University (Amendment) Act 2026 at its ninth meeting....

item.title: World Environment Day today
item.description: World Environment Day-2026 will be observed in the country, as elsewhere across the globe, with a view to raising awareness about environmental protection on Friday....

item.title: Outgoing ICT division secretary accorded farewell reception
item.description: A farewell reception was held on Thursday in honour of Kazi Anwar Hossain, outgoing secretary of the Information and Communication Technology Division, on the occasion of his transfer....

item.title: Opposition parties protest against power price hikes
item.description: Different opposition political parties on Thursday staged rallies and processions in Dhaka and Rajshahi protesting against the latest increase in power prices....

item.title: Sharif Jamil elected as Asia representative to Waterkeeper Alliance Global Board
item.description: Sharif Jamil, Coordinator of Waterkeepers Bangladesh, has been elected as the representative for the Asia region to the Global Board of the Waterkeeper Alliance. The announcement was made during the organisation’s 2026 Annual General Meeting, held virtually on 3 June 2026 at 10:00am (US time)....

item.title: PM seeks partners’ support for Bangladesh development
item.description: Prime minister Tarique Rahman on Thursday sought continued support from development partners for Bangladesh’s overall development and sustainable progress as United Nations Development Programme resident representative in Bangladesh Stefan Liller paid a courtesy call on him on Thursday....
